arguments.task: Open the LinkedIn Marketing developer documentation on Microsoft Learn and read official first-party pages needed to determine: one-line product description, documented authentication methods, access gating or program requirements, API surface type and breadth, rate limit notes, and any official MCP/server offering. Prefer canonical Microsoft Learn and official LinkedIn/Microsoft pages. Return a structured summary with the exact page URLs visited and concise excerpts supporting each field. Also open any linked official auth, development, versioning, and rate limit pages that are necessary. Do not rely on search engine snippets.

raw_result.description: LinkedIn Ads provides LinkedIn Marketing APIs for managing B2B advertising, leads, conversions, and related marketing workflows on LinkedIn.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official LinkedIn Marketing/Microsoft Learn docs document OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow for API access, with access tokens valid for 60 days and authorization codes valid for 30 minutes. The marketing access page also states some products use two-legged OAuth for permissions, but the canonical auth page reviewed documents OAuth 2.0 rather than API keys, Basic auth, or personal access tokens.
raw_result.gating_reason: LinkedIn states API products require application and approval. Advertising API and some other products are open to approved developers at Development Tier, while higher tiers and several related marketing products require additional qualification review and manual access increases.

api_surface.breadth: REST APIs under the LinkedIn Marketing platform cover advertising campaign management, creatives, analytics, lead sync, conversions, event/community management, and some additional gated marketing datasets and planning products.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official MCP or Model Context Protocol server offering was found in the official Microsoft Learn LinkedIn Marketing documentation reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: A first toolkit is possible with public REST docs and OAuth 2.0, but production-relevant LinkedIn Ads access is gated by developer application approval and some capabilities require manual tier increases or additional qualification.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: LinkedIn documents both application-level and member-level rate limits. Limits reset daily at midnight UTC, 429 is returned when exceeded, and concrete quota values are available in the developer portal's Analytics tab rather than published as static numbers.
raw_result.notable_signals: The platform uses versioned REST endpoints with a required Linkedin-Version header and monthly version cadence; Advertising API and Community Management API expose Development vs Standard tiers, showing a usable but approval-mediated path for agent tooling.
